topic: Mag-gravity coupling constant kappa = 1.67 nT/uGal — c4 post-patch review
change_summary: Two c3-recommended major additions were applied (ISS-1171 dome reconciliation quote; ISS-1172 Wang timing critique). detail_evidence grew 4061->5190 (+28%). detail_verdict_text unchanged (1351). Fresh adversarial pass on the new text.
our_argument_summary: WIN-012 now includes (a) a direct quote of the dome's own reconciliation of WIN-013 — "Confirms electromagnetic, not gravitational" — and (b) a timing critique attributed to Wang et al. (2000) stating the gravity valleys were at first/last contact, not at eclipse maximum, with the phrase "no significant anomaly during the very solar eclipse was found". Both additions strengthen the self-contradiction verdict. However, on fresh reading, both quotes have attribution problems (see holes_found).

relevant_files: data/wins.json WIN-011 entry (cites both Wang 2000 and Yang & Wang 2002)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Unchanged from c3. kappa_obs = 10.9/6.5 = 1.677 nT/uGal compared to hardcoded KAPPA = 1.67. Tautological identity check; no external fetch. c3 analysis stands.
data_sources_used: No API calls in monitor.py Domain #7 for WIN-012
Cited upstream: Wang et al. 2000 Phys. Rev. D 62, 041101; Yang & Wang 2002 Astrophys. Space Sci. 282, 245; INTERMAGNET BOU 2017; Van Camp et al. 2001; Sun et al. 2010
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The dome's LIVE-SITE WIN-013 framing is now "This result cannot discriminate between the models" — a much more defensible escape than the older "Confirms electromagnetic, not gravitational" phrasing our review quotes. The dome has moved off the self-contradictory framing we attack.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: The core code_analysis of monitor.py Domain #7 is unchanged and remains accurate. The new discrepancies are in prose citations and quote attributions, not code claims.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Unchanged from c3. monitor.py Domain #7 hardcodes KAPPA = 1.67 and compares to hardcoded 10.9/6.5 = 1.677. No fetch.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Unchanged from c3. Chapman 1933 ionospheric Sq explains the magnetic side; SG nulls (Van Camp 2001, Sun 2010) explain the gravity side as null. Dome relabels a null+ionospheric pair as "coupling".
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Unchanged from c3. BOU 2017 and Mohe 1997 are both pre-existing literature values; ratio computed after the fact.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Unchanged from c3. No dome-geometry derivation of 1.67 exists; the value is a ratio of adopted measurements.

holes_found.description: Direct-quote source may be stale or paraphrased. The newly added sentence directly quotes the dome as describing WIN-013 as "Confirms electromagnetic, not gravitational". But a fresh WebFetch of the LIVE dome site (https://john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html on 2026-04-20) returns a different WIN-013 entry: "Honest status: The SG null is consistent with both the globe model (no mechanism for eclipse gravity changes) AND the ECM pipeline prediction (Chebychev low-pass attenuates contact-phase transients before Level-3 release). This result cannot discriminate between the models." The phrase "Confirms electromagnetic, not gravitational" appears in our raw-text/02-wins.txt (dated 2026-03-12, over a month old) and may be either (a) a paraphrased summary our analyst wrote during extraction, or (b) accurate text from an older dome site version that has since been revised. Either way, a dome defender checking the current site will not find the quote and will (correctly) accuse us of quoting text the dome no longer publishes. If the dome author reads our review, he can point at the current "cannot discriminate between the models" framing and say "that is not what I claim and never was." This is a CRITICAL citation-integrity issue — the same class of error we criticize the dome for (WIN-008/009/014 had wrong DOIs; we now have a quote that may not exist on the source).
holes_found.recommendation: Before keeping this quote, verify it exists on the LIVE dome site or in the Wayback Machine snapshot for a specific date. If confirmed historical-only, reframe as: "The dome's earlier WIN-013 framing (captured in our raw-text extract dated 2026-03-12) described the null as 'Confirms electromagnetic, not gravitational' — the current site has been softened to 'cannot discriminate between the models', but our critique still applies: if the null cannot discriminate between models, the Mohe -6.5 uGal cannot be elevated to a confirmed gravity anomaly either." If no historical version contains the quote, drop it entirely and rewrite the paragraph using the conditional logic (which does NOT depend on a direct dome quote). Trigger a poller re-scrape of wins.html and update raw-text/ to current.

holes_found.description: Quote misattribution — Wang et al. (2000) vs Yang & Wang (2002). Our new text attributes "no significant anomaly during the very solar eclipse was found" and the "valleys at first/last contact" framing to Wang et al. (2000). WebSearch and ADS WebFetch (2026-04-20) both confirm this exact phrasing appears in the abstract of Yang, X-S. & Wang, Q-S. (2002), Astrophys. Space Sci. 282, 245 — the reanalysis paper — not in Wang et al. (2000) Phys. Rev. D 62, 041101. The 2000 paper abstract (per search) says "The observed anomaly was (7.0+/-2.7)x10^-8 m/s^2 during the eclipse, implying that there may be a shielding property of gravitation" — the 2000 paper CLAIMED a shielding-consistent signal. It was the 2002 reanalysis that walked back to "no significant anomaly... valleys at contacts." WIN-011 has the same misattribution (it says "Wang et al. (2000) ... The paper explicitly states" and then adds Yang & Wang 2002 as a separate "subsequent reanalysis"). A dome defender or a fair-minded physicist who fetches the 2000 paper will find no such quote, will find instead the shielding claim, and will accuse us of fabricating a citation.
holes_found.recommendation: Re-attribute the quote to Yang & Wang (2002). Suggested rewrite of the timing sentence in WIN-012 detail_evidence: "Even the -6.5 uGal value itself is suspect on the dome's own cited sources: Yang & Wang's own 2002 reanalysis (Astrophys. Space Sci. 282, 245 — co-authored by the lead Mohe observer) states 'no significant anomaly during the very solar eclipse was found' and describes the signal as 'two gravity anomaly valleys with near-symmetrical decrease of about 6-7 uGal at the first contact and the last contact' — opposite to what gravitational shielding would predict (peak at maximum coverage). See WIN-011." Also apply the same fix to WIN-011 detail_evidence (parallel error, not in scope of this queue item but file a companion issue).

holes_found.description: The dome's LIVE WIN-011 entry already cites Yang & Wang 2002 and titles the claim "CONFIRMED (Mechanism Contested)", and the dome already concedes "shielding ruled out, thermal tilt loophole remains open" (per WebFetch 2026-04-20). Our WIN-012 attack treats the dome as if it still claims gravitational shielding; the current dome position is weaker — the dome admits shielding is dead and has retreated to a "thermal tilt loophole." A dome defender will say: "We already conceded what you're arguing. Your critique is fighting a position we've abandoned." This doesn't rescue kappa (which still conflates an EM-plus-thermal effect with gravity), but we should acknowledge the concession and redirect the attack: the dome's own retreat to a non-gravitational explanation FOR WIN-011 means kappa's denominator is — by the dome's current framing — not a gravity anomaly at all.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a short paragraph to detail_evidence after the "circular" paragraph: "The dome's current WIN-011 entry itself concedes that 'shielding is ruled out' and labels the mechanism 'contested', retreating to a thermal-tilt loophole. But that concession is fatal to kappa: if the -6.5 uGal is not a gravity anomaly from eclipse shielding but a thermal-tilt artifact or an EM effect on the gravimeter sensor, then the denominator of a 'magnetic-gravity coupling constant' is not a gravity measurement at all — and kappa cannot be a coupling constant between magnetism and gravity." This turns the dome's own retreat into ammunition.

holes_found.description: Attribution phrasing "the raw-text source notes describe" is internally meaningless to readers. "Raw-text source notes" refers to files inside our own review repository. A reader has no idea what this means — it reads as if we are quoting our own internal notes, not the dome site. Even if the quote itself were verified on the live site, this attribution phrasing weakens the rhetorical force of "pin the dome with its own words" — the whole point of the c3 recommendation was to quote the DOME, not our mirror of the dome.
holes_found.recommendation: Change "the raw-text source notes describe WIN-013/014 as" to either (a) "the dome's own source statement for WIN-013 describes the null as" (if the quote is verified on the live site), or (b) drop the direct quote and use paraphrase with citation to the specific dome page URL and access date. Avoid referring to our own internal files in user-facing prose.

holes_found.description: Charitability tension on the -6.5 uGal value. Our text first accepts -6.5 uGal as the kappa denominator ("kappa = -10.9 nT / 6.5 uGal = 1.677"), then later attacks the value as suspect via Wang/Yang timing critique. A careful defender will say: "You can't both use the value for your arithmetic and reject it as unreliable. Pick a lane." The logically clean version is: accept the value arguendo for the kappa computation, then show that EVEN accepting it, the ratio fails because (a) the denominator is now known to be from contact-phase artifact, not eclipse-maximum shielding, and (b) the dome's own WIN-013 null contradicts the denominator being nonzero more broadly.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a single clarifying phrase at the start of the Wang timing sentence: "Even granting the dome its preferred numerical value for the sake of argument, the reported timing contradicts the shielding interpretation: ..." This explicitly marks the charitable reading and prevents the "having it both ways" objection.

holes_found.description: Cross-WIN link anchor verified correct. The c3 review recommended #win-011 but the current text uses #win011 (no hyphen). The generate-html.js script produces anchor ids of the form win{win.id} = "win011" — so #win011 is correct and #win-011 would have been broken. This is noted as VERIFIED CORRECT, not a hole — flagging here only because c3's recommendation was mildly wrong on this point and a future reviewer following c3 literally would introduce a broken anchor.
holes_found.recommendation: No action. The current text is correct. Noting here so future reviewers do not inadvertently "fix" this to #win-011.

holes_found.description: Dome raw-text line 90 reads: "Source: Van Camp 1999. 0.0 uGal. Confirms electromagnetic, not gravitational." Note two issues: (a) the citation says "Van Camp 1999" but Van Camp et al. (2001) is the published reference — this is a date error in our raw-text mirror (the 1999 date is the eclipse year, not the publication year); (b) the compact format suggests this line is a paraphrase/summary our analyst wrote during extraction, not a verbatim dome-site copy. This confirms the critical/major concern above — the quote attribution is shakier than c3 recognized.
holes_found.recommendation: Schedule a raw-text refresh. The 2026-03-12 extraction is now five weeks old and the dome has updated. File a tinker/poller task to re-extract raw-text/ and add a sanity-check script that diffs raw-text vs live-site HTML.

stronger_arguments.description: Dome concession leverage. The LIVE dome site explicitly marks WIN-011 as "(Mechanism Contested)" and admits "shielding ruled out, thermal tilt loophole remains open." This is a SIGNIFICANT concession — more useful than the old "electromagnetic, not gravitational" framing — and directly attacks kappa: if WIN-011's mechanism is "thermal tilt" (instrument artifact) rather than gravity, kappa's denominator is not a gravity measurement.

Yang & Wang 2002 as the correct citation for the valleys-at-contact timing critique. This paper is co-authored by Qian-Shen Wang (lead observer on the 2000 paper), so we are not pitting outsiders against insiders — we are quoting the observer's own reanalysis to correct the observer's own earlier interpretation. Rhetorically stronger than just citing the 2000 paper.
sources: Yang, X-S. & Wang, Q-S. 2002, Astrophys. Space Sci. 282, 245-253, DOI 10.1023/A:1021119023985

stronger_arguments.description: Unnikrishnan et al. (2001) Phys. Rev. D 63, 062002 — "Anomalous gravity data during the 1997 total solar eclipse do not support the hypothesis of gravitational shielding" — is a third independent paper that directly refutes the shielding interpretation. Our WIN-012 currently does not cite Unnikrishnan. Adding this citation would make the rejection of shielding mechanism broader and more bulletproof (we rely on Van Camp nulls, Sun nulls, and the observer's own reanalysis — adding Unnikrishnan makes it four independent refutations, not three).
sources: Unnikrishnan et al. 2001, Phys. Rev. D 63, 062002, DOI 10.1103/PhysRevD.63.062002

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome has quietly improved WIN-011's framing since our last full review — retreating from "confirmed gravity anomaly" to "CONFIRMED (Mechanism Contested) — shielding ruled out, thermal tilt loophole." This is an epistemically honest move: the dome is updating on evidence rather than digging in. We should credit this in passing to signal that our review is tracking the current dome position, not a strawman.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: A retreat to "mechanism contested" destroys kappa as a "magnetic-gravity coupling." If the Mohe signal is thermal-tilt artifact rather than gravity, the coupling is between magnetism and a thermal-tilt artifact — which is not a physical coupling constant. The dome's concession is partial (it still displays WIN-011 as CONFIRMED and still uses -6.5 uGal for kappa); the concession is incomplete enough to keep the WIN-012 self-contradiction alive, but it also means our attack must be updated to hit the current position, not the old one.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A capable dome defender, reading the c3+ current text, could say three things in sequence: (1) "You quote 'Confirms electromagnetic, not gravitational' but that phrase is not on the current site — check https://john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html today." (2) "You attribute a timing quote to Wang et al. (2000) Phys. Rev. D — that paper claims shielding. The quote you want is from Yang & Wang (2002) — please cite the correct paper." (3) "Our current WIN-011 entry already concedes 'shielding ruled out' — your review attacks a position we've updated. Bring your critique forward to our current wording and we'll engage." All three rebuttals are factually correct and could, in combination, make our WIN-012 critique look sloppy and outdated — even though the CORE argument (kappa is circular / self-contradictory / conflates a null with a non-null) still stands.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Apply all three fixes proposed in holes_found[0], [1], and [2] in a single coordinated patch. After the patch, the WIN-012 critique will (a) be grounded in verified live-site text rather than stale paraphrase, (b) cite the correct paper for the valleys-at-contact timing critique, and (c) engage with the dome's current "mechanism contested" framing rather than the older "confirms electromagnetic" framing. The verdict ("Self-Contradicted") still holds — it is actually strengthened, because the dome's own retreat to "thermal tilt" is a cleaner refutation of kappa than the older electromagnetic framing.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-011 and WIN-012 both misattribute the Yang & Wang (2002) quote to Wang et al. (2000). WIN-011's detail_evidence says: "Wang et al. (2000, Phys. Rev. D) ... The paper explicitly states 'no significant anomaly during the very solar eclipse was found.'" — but that phrase is from the 2002 reanalysis. When we import this quote into WIN-012 (per c3 recommendation), we inherit the citation error. Fixing WIN-012 without fixing WIN-011 creates a NEW inconsistency between the two entries.
cross_win_consistency.notes: Recommend: (a) fix WIN-012 per holes_found above; (b) file a companion issue to fix WIN-011 in the same patch window so the two entries stay consistent; (c) consider whether any other WIN inherits "Wang et al. 2000" + that specific quote (grep data/wins.json for the string — we should only have it in WIN-011 and WIN-012, but verify).

claims_checked: Wang et al. 2000 DOI 10.1103/PhysRevD.62.041101 resolves to "Precise measurement of gravity variations during a total solar eclipse" by Wang, Yang, Wu, Guo, Liu, Hua — verified via WebSearch 2026-04-20
Yang & Wang 2002 published in Astrophys. Space Sci. 282, 245 — verified via ADS (2002Ap&SS.282..245Y)
The phrase "no significant anomaly during the very solar eclipse was found" appears in Yang & Wang 2002 abstract — verified via ADS WebFetch 2026-04-20

The phrase "two gravity anomaly valleys with near-symmetrical decrease of about 6-7 uGal at the first contact and the last contact" appears in Yang & Wang 2002 abstract — verified via ADS WebFetch 2026-04-20

Live dome site WIN-013 does NOT contain "Confirms electromagnetic, not gravitational" — verified via WebFetch https://john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html on 2026-04-20. Current site reads "Honest status: ... cannot discriminate between the models."

Live dome site WIN-011 now titled "CONFIRMED (Mechanism Contested)" citing both Wang 2000 and Yang & Wang 2002, with "shielding ruled out, thermal tilt loophole remains open" — verified via WebFetch 2026-04-20
quantitative_verification.computations: kappa arithmetic unchanged (10.9/6.5 = 1.677, 0.4% from KAPPA = 1.67). Fingerprint delta: detail_evidence 4061 -> 5190 = +1129 chars = +27.8%. Matches expected +2 paragraph delta from ISS-1171 + ISS-1172 patches.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Two citation-integrity failures: (1) the "Confirms electromagnetic, not gravitational" quote is not on the current dome site (possibly from an older/paraphrased raw-text); (2) the "no significant anomaly during the very solar eclipse was found" quote is from Yang & Wang (2002), not Wang et al. (2000) as attributed. Both are fixable with targeted text edits; neither overturns the "Self-Contradicted" verdict.

deeper_analysis: Cycle 4 is the first fresh adversarial pass after the c3 recommendations (ISS-1171, ISS-1172) were applied. The two additions are substantively correct in logic but introduce two avoidable citation errors and one stale-source risk, all of which a careful dome defender could turn into a "your review is sloppy" rejoinder. The core verdict (Self-Contradicted) survives because the kappa self-contradiction argument does not depend on either of the new quotes — it rests on the WIN-013/014 null results (independently verified via Van Camp 2001 and Sun 2010) and the hardcoded-identity-check structure of monitor.py Domain #7. However, the review as currently written is MORE vulnerable to surface-level rebuttal than the c3 version was, because two new direct quotes now invite citation-check counterattacks. The fix is straightforward: re-attribute the Yang/Wang quote, verify or reframe the "electromagnetic not gravitational" quote against the live site, and acknowledge the dome's retreat to "mechanism contested." Doing all three in a single patch yields a version that is both rhetorically stronger and citation-safer. Advocate-mode defense now rates 3/5 (up from 2/5 in c3) specifically because of the citation errors; after fixes, it should return to 2/5 or below.

citations_verified: Wang et al. 2000 DOI 10.1103/PhysRevD.62.041101 (paper exists; authors and title confirmed)
Van Camp et al. 2001 DOI 10.1023/A:1013940928637 (carried forward from c3, no change)
Yang & Wang 2002, Astrophys. Space Sci. 282, 245, DOI 10.1023/A:1021119023985 (ADS verified 2026-04-20)
INTERMAGNET and JPL PO.DAAC URLs (carried forward from c3)
citations_failed: Direct quote "no significant anomaly during the very solar eclipse was found" attributed to Wang et al. (2000) — actually Yang & Wang (2002). MISATTRIBUTION, not a fabricated citation — the quote is real but from a different paper.

Direct quote "Confirms electromagnetic, not gravitational" attributed to the dome's raw-text source notes — not present on live dome site (https://john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html WebFetched 2026-04-20). May be historical or paraphrased; cannot be verified against current live text.
citations_unchecked: Unnikrishnan et al. 2001 Phys. Rev. D 63, 062002 — mentioned in stronger_arguments as a potential addition; DOI resolves but full paper not fetched in this review
Sun et al. 2010 — inherited from c3, not re-checked

next_review_trigger: Re-review after the three major/critical holes are patched. Specifically: (a) raw-text refresh + verification of electromagnetic-not-gravitational quote against current site or Wayback snapshot; (b) re-attribution of the timing quote to Yang & Wang 2002 in BOTH WIN-011 and WIN-012; (c) addition of dome-concession acknowledgment paragraph. If any of these are left unpatched, the critique remains vulnerable on surface-level citation checks.
